name: Typewriter Phrase / Keyword Font-Shuffle
one_line: Words type in one-per-onset to spell a phrase, then one keyword cycles typefaces on the beat while everything else holds dead still.

match.notes: Deliberately NOT gated on energy_level — the discriminator is a steady grid with a CONTINUOUS onset stream (no desert). This is the inverse of held-message-living-field, which requires onset_gap_bars >= 1.5 (an onset desert) and energy >= MEDIUM. If the grid goes quiet (gap >= ~1.5 bars), use a hold template instead.

lead_phrase.notes: 1-3 words; one word pops in per hi-hat onset and ACCUMULATES (typewriter, not replace), then holds. group_2: growth@0.557 · through@0.906.

keyword.notes: PROPOSED NEW motion verb. The final word types in (base face) then cycles through the FONTS[] preset library on each subsequent beat/onset — 0ms hard swaps; holds the base face between flips and after the last one. group_2: types @1.231, period @1.440, shuffles @1.904/2.392/2.624/3.088.

background.notes: Pure black in this reference impl (per the reverse brief). The original group_2 layered a glitch/strobe/RGB-split field here (motion: strobe / energy_envelope_breath) — an OPTIONAL add-on role, not required by the structural type.

beat_map.comment: Script-side constants in index.html, filled from <group>.audiomap.json. Content lives in params; musical timing lives here.
